Phase-51.NAME: Favicon and Resend Email Logo Branding
Phase-51.intent: Add KrotPN favicon assets to browser surfaces and add the prepared KrotPN logo inside Resend HTML emails without placing visible logos in the current frontend UI.

scope.in: Frontend favicon/PWA assets, admin favicon assets, public email logo asset, verification/reset email HTML logo rendering, README operator notes, and static smoke verification.
scope.out: Visible site/cabinet/admin UI logos, frontend redesign, BIMI DNS changes, Resend provider API behavior, install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, nginx edge topology, VPN runtime, and MTProto runtime.

steps.step-1: Select prepared PNG assets from /home/truffle/Загрузки/ДЛЯ КРОТА/logos and materialize favicon, PWA, Apple touch, and email-logo assets in frontend/admin public surfaces.
steps.step-2: Wire user frontend index.html and Vite PWA includeAssets to real favicon and icon filenames.
steps.step-3: Wire admin frontend index.html and public assets to the same favicon identity without adding visible UI logo components.
steps.step-4: Render the public brand logo URL in Resend HTML email templates while keeping text fallbacks and token redaction unchanged.
steps.step-5: Preserve verification/reset onboarding behavior while adding email-logo presentation to the shared action-email layout.
steps.step-6: Add Phase-51 smoke gate for asset dimensions, HTML references, email template binding, README notes, and protected deploy/install surfaces.
steps.step-7: Run backend email tests, frontend/admin builds, Phase-51 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy guard.
steps.step-8: Update execution evidence and current-status after verification passes.

acceptance.metric-1: Browsers can request favicon.ico plus 16/32 PNG fallbacks from user and admin frontend builds.
acceptance.metric-2: User frontend PWA build includes apple-touch-icon.png, pwa-192x192.png, and pwa-512x512.png.
acceptance.metric-3: Verification and password reset emails include an HTTPS logo URL derived from FRONTEND_URL and do not add the logo URL to text fallback content.
acceptance.metric-4: README clearly separates email-template logo rendering from provider-side BIMI sender avatar behavior.
acceptance.metric-5: install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, nginx edge topology, VPN, and MTProto runtime remain untouched.

risk-controls.risk-1: Email clients may block remote images by default; this phase only guarantees HTML template presence and public asset delivery.
risk-controls.risk-2: Favicon caches can be sticky in browsers; standard root filenames are used for compatibility.
risk-controls.risk-3: The visible frontend redesign is intentionally deferred, so no cabinet/admin layout logo is introduced here.

execution-evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-02: backend/tests/test_email_delivery.py passed 5/5 with email-logo HTML assertions and token redaction preserved.
execution-evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-02: frontend npm run build and frontend-admin npm run build passed; Phase-51 smoke with --expect-dist confirmed public assets copied into dist.
execution-evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-02: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install diff guard passed.
